The boy completely froze when he saw Shuichi. “Uh…” He looked around like Shuichi was the weird one in this situation? This was his bedroom!“Is that my math book?” Shuichi asked, now looking incredibly confused, gesturing to the book in the boy's other hand. There was an extremely long pause, and for a moment, Shuichi thought he might not say anything at all.“No…?” He finally spoke up, looking at the book for a second, and then back up at Shuichi. Shuichi stared at the boy, trying to grasp the situation properly.“It has my name on it.” That made the boy once again look back at the book, reading Shuichi Saihara neatly written in sharpie on the cover.“Oh, well look at that.” The boy shrugged.“What exactly are you doing?” Shuichi looked at the large bag that the boy was holding.“Great question!” The boy chirped, tossing the book into the bag. “I’m…Uh…”Shuichi sighed. “It looks like you’re robbing me.”

title: Secrecy and sex
author: girlsgogames
fandoms: New Dangan Ronpa V3: Everyone's New Semester of Killing
warnings: Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings
tags: Oma Kokichi Is Bad at Feelings
summary:
Oma Kokichi hadn't been the luckiest. Actually, you could say he's the most unfortunate person in this univeristy. For all he knows anyway. He's young, inexperienced, but jaded as all hell, sarcasm being his first language.
He's never had much: an abusive mother, a father he never knew. Of course, that meant he was the one who had to make ends meet. He barely scraped the grades to get into his uni, not having time to juggle both a job and school. Even so, a loan free first year encouraged him enough to smile, even a little bit.
He was free after all, moving to another city to persue his dream: politics. He'd always wanted a voice, and he was gonna give himself one. The question is, how far could he go? How much running do you have to do to escape your past?
He might have the will to lie right now, but when he meets a cute detective, the truth he swallowed will practically be giving him acid reflux. Heartburn. And suddenly, he's not so sure about anything anymore.

summary:
“Delirious, that’s what I must be now, because despite the inescapable fact that I would soon pass away, my heart couldn’t help but burn, burn, though not because my core was the place she has stabbed me, not because I was nervous, but burn like a fire with the way her attention was solely on me. ‘Finally,’ the selfish thought intruded my head. ‘I’m the only one she’s looking at.’”
“Inane. That is what this situation is. It must be, because this certainly is ridiculous. Why dost this heart of mine beat faster as I watch her breaths even out? What warrants such a reaction from me? It’s burning, but I’m unsure of what. It certainly can’t be from injury, nor nervousness (‘tis a weak emotion.), but the way her eyes follow me… ‘Why,’ the misplaced thought intruded my head. ‘is she looking at me that way?’”
Thoughts spin like a whirlwind even in death, so resolve thine mind concurrently.
OR
Two perspectives, two different people. Both stuck in a killing game, one decides the other’s life was worth sacrificing.

summary:
Omegas are the only dynamic capable of childbearing. Omegas, also, comprise only 5% of the Japanese population. Although the reasons for their abruptly low numbers were unexplained by science, the consequences were clear to all-- and with birth rates plummeting to catastrophic levels, the people grew desperate. Spurred by the words of one charismatic leader, they decided that the survival of society was more important than the wellbeing of the vulnerable. So it went, for decades.
Saihara Shuichi had a normal life. He worked part-time at his uncle's detective agency. He had dreams of getting a degree and becoming a detective himself. Most importantly, he thought he was a Beta. But one night, after a strange encounter with a runaway omega, he comes home...different. Multiple years late, he presents as an omega himself. No longer permitted at his high school, he's transferred to a dreary boarding academy in the rural woods of Honshu, where he has to learn how to fulfill his new, sole duty to the country; as a mate and father. Where no one is there to hear him, except for the ghosts of long-extinct wolves.Oh, and the runaway is there too.
OR: Saihara and Ouma suffer through omegaverse misogyny and kiss.

He knew that the people who walked in that door would think “Momota Kaito is dead. Ouma Kokichi killed him.”
He remembered snorting then when that thought came, and he remembered that he thought, “They could never be more wrong. But let them think that.”

This Has Been a WIP for Months... Oh My God
summary:
Harukawa Maki is unused to special attention. Her entire life, she's been a part of a herd—just another cog, another solider headed to slaughter. To her total frustration, this flips on its head and she quickly becomes the object of one Chikako Atsuko's affection. She hates it. She insists on hating the attention, in fact. She does everything she knows how to do to get her to stop—every scare tactic, every threat. It doesn't take. And, slowly, she finds herself liking the attention she swears to hate.
But then Momota dies, and Chikako spills the alibi keeping her from pinning Ouma as the killer, and all the affection is gone—drained like blood from the carotid. Instantaneous.
So, when she notices the sickly pallor of her skin that morning, while her mind runs a mile a minute—betraying her in its concern, she doesn't do a double take. Instead, she storms out of the cafeteria and doesn't look back.
How she wishes she would've looked back.

summary:
Andrew and Ashley Graves are hired by an old detective librarian Kirigiri Fuhito, a man known for his dangerous and extreme ideology. Driven by pride and personal motives, Fuhito promises to pay them double, offering millions of yen for their work. Since the siblings will only be staying in Japan for half a year, they leave their children behind to watch over their home and butcher shop.

“Oh my goodness! Are you alright?!”
Kokichi froze at the familiar voice, entire body tensing. Hesitantly, he glanced up and was met with the sight of Kiibo looking down at him with concern.
What the fuck.
Kokichi felt dizzy. He looked around, noticing their surroundings was…one of the classrooms. He whipped his head around, looking back at the locker he just fell out of. He was in the locker…just like at the beginning of the game.
Again, what the fuck.
Or, Kokichi dies for his plan and then wakes up back at the start. So, naturally, he has to think of a new course of action…which ends up requiring cooperation, trust, and a lot less lying to pull off, much to Kokichi’s displeasure.
